title: SectorPack
description: A curated, sector-specific bundle of frameworks (required / recommended / optional), a pre-computed crosswalk subset relevant to those frameworks, and sequencing notes. Sector packs are starting points for scoping a programme — not legal advice. See packages/ontology/LEGAL_DISCLAIMER.md and packages/ontology/SECTOR_PACKS.md.

sector_pack_id.description: Stable, kebab-case slug identifying the sector pack (e.g. 'fintech-eu').

description.description: One- or two-sentence description of who this pack is for.

version.description: SemVer of the sector-pack definition itself.

required_frameworks.description: Frameworks the customer must implement to operate in this sector.

recommended_frameworks.description: Frameworks the customer should adopt as best practice, or which become required under common sub-cases (e.g. 'if AI/ML').

optional_frameworks.description: Frameworks that apply only in narrow sub-cases (e.g. 'if handling PHI').

rationale.description: Why this pair is pre-mapped for this sector — points the reader at the highest-leverage shared clauses.

crosswalk_pairs.description: Pre-computed crosswalk subset relevant to this sector. Each pair MUST correspond to an actual edge in packages/frameworks/src/crosswalks.ts.

sequencing_notes.description: Ordered guidance on which frameworks to stand up first and why (typically because later frameworks reuse earlier evidence).

last_reviewed.description: ISO-8601 date this pack was last reviewed by a domain SME.
